rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE JOAN GRAYSON REVOCABLE TRUST DATED JULY 12, 1994
rulings.Calendar Matter: Verified Petition To: 1) Contest Validity Of The Purported Restatement Due To Lack Of Capacity; 2) Contest Validity Of The Purported Restatement Due To Undue Influence 3) Find That Respondent Committed Elder Financial Abuse Against Decedent; And 4) Recover Double Damages And Attorneys' Fees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) No proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] Petitioners' counsel refuses to provide a proposed order, claiming that one will be presented "following the outcome of the court hearing(s)." 2) The proof of service shows that JC form DE-120, not DE-115, was mailed. Part of the relief sought is for double damages under Probate Code Section 859. 3) Any party lodging a proposed order in a contested matter must file proof of service of the proposed order on all parties entitled to notice of the petition. [LRSF 14.7 A.4.] The proof of service does not indicate that copies of the petition and a proposed order were served. Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE SURVIVOR'S TRUST OF THE 2003 SCHOLTEN FAMILY TRUST DATED DECEMBER 4, 2003 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Instructions
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required, despite the following issues that have not been addressed by the objectors: 1) Both objectors must sign their opposition. See Probate Code section 1020 & CRC 7.103(a). 2) The opposition must be verified. See section 1021(a)(2) & CRC 7.103(b). 3) Stacey Scholten's declaration must be signed under penalty of perjury in compliance with CCP section 2015.5. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE SURVIVOR'S TRUST OF THE 2003 SCHOLTEN FAMILY TRUST DATED DECEMBER 4, 2003 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Removal Of Lisabeth Scholten As Trustee Of The 2003 Scholten Family Trust Appointment Of Receiver Or Temporary Trustee And An Accounting And Exhibits In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required, despite the following issues: 1) Both petitioners must sign the petition. See Probate Code section 1020 & CRC 7.103(a). 2) The petition must be verified. See section 1021(a)(1) & CRC 7.103(b). 3) The name and address of each beneficiary of the trust must be alleged in the petition. See section 17201 & CRC 7.902. 4) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-120 form at least 30 days before the hearing. Note: A copy of the proposed order must be served on each person entitled to notice in a contested matter. Proof of service must be filed as a separate document (not as an attachment to the proposed order merely lodged). See LRSF 14.7(A)(4). PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Petition For Letters Of Administration
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. Status of closing the estate will be set for 3/24/26. The administrator must file either a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report of status of administration pursuant to Prob. C. Section 12200 (requesting additional time for estate administration) at least two weeks before 3/224/26. Appearance will be required on that status date if no petition has been filed. (Courtesy copies and a hard copy proposed order would have been appreciated. [LRSF 14.7 A., B., and C. and 14.59 E.])

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JANIE KATHLEEN LINDSAY BRAY
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 04/16/2025, as nothing has been filed to address the following issues (see tentative ruling for 02/19/2025 hearing: The proof of publication on file does not show the petitioner's request for IAEA authority was published. New notice must be published if the petitioner continues to seek IAEA authority (see item 2c of the petition requesting full authority). In addition, the following issues (e-mailed to the petitioner on 01/06/2025) remain unaddressed: 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged. 2) No DE-147 form is on file. 3) No DE-147S form is on file. A pleading cover sheet must be attached to this form. 4) No DE-121 form is on file. A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the form at least 15 days before the hearing. 5) Item 2d(2) of the petition: The bond amount must be alleged. For full IAEA authority, the amount must match the amount alleged at item 3d(7). 6) Item 3a: Was the decedent domiciled in San Francisco County, despite her place of death in Burlingame, San Mateo County? 7) Item 3d(5): Is any real property subject to encumbrances (e.g., mortgage, line of credit, other lien)? If so, the total balance must be alleged. 8) Item 5a(3) or (4) (at least one box) must be checked. 9) Item 5a(7) or (8) (at least one box) must be checked. Note: An appearance may be required on 04/16/2025 for the Court to determine whether to take this petition off calendar if papers showing satisfactory progress to address the issues are not filed.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF TORIBIO RICARDO MARTINEZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Court to determine whether the presumption in Probate Code Section 6124 applies and whether to waive bond. Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE CHAMBERLAIN REVOCABLE TRUST DATED OCTOBER 9,1997
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petitioon To Allow Modification Of Trust (Probate Code Sections 15409 And 17200)
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 04/22/2025. Issues necessitating a continuance: 1) Notice is insufficient. 30-day notice is required (Probate Code Section 17203). The notice of hearing shows service of the petition occured on 02/13/2025, just 26 days prior to the 03/11/2025 hearing date. 2) All notices must include language about remote appearances (San Francisco Local Rule 14.1.C.6). Please address the above issues and deliver courtesy copies of all filed documents to this examiner no later than 04/08/2025 to avoid further continuance.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE 1993 BORSUK FAMILY TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: (1) Compelling Trustee To Distribute Trust Assets To Beneficiaries; (2) Barring Trustee'S Use Of Trust Funds For Litigation; And (3) Awarding
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/5/25 to be heard by the judicial officer who heard the related case. (No earlier availability.) Notice of Hearing must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as information about remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (A pleading form of proof of service is not necessary; the proof of service on the reverse side of the mandatory Judicial Council form Notice of Hearing is the proof of service that sufficiently shows which notice was mailed and when, etc.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MALCOLM EBAUGH
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 04/01/2025 for the following issues to be addressed: 1) Item 1 of the DE-121 form must include the decedent's first name "Malcolm". New notice of the continued hearing must be mailed and published. 2) The proof of publication on file does not show the decedent's first name "Malcolm". New notice must be published. 3) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-121 form at least 15 days before the hearing. New notice must be mailed with a copy of the remote appearance information. 4) Please provide the name and date of death of the decedent's sibling who was the parent of each nephew and niece listed at item 8 of the petition. 5) Did David Ebaugh leave issue? 6) The decedent died in 2003. Please briefly explain why the petitioner delayed opening this probate. Note: Please provide an e-mail address if counsel wants to receive notes from the assigned examiner via e-mail.
